candidates.name: J.B. Pritzker
candidates.notes: JB Pritzker scores 2/70 — near the bottom. Billionaire heir to Hyatt Hotels fortune. Jewish faith of Ukrainian descent. Instrumental in building Illinois Holocaust Museum. Most progressive governor in this set — signed abortion rights into state constitution, assault weapons ban, sanctuary state law. Net worth $3.9B makes him wealthiest governor in America. Eyeing 2028 presidential run. Announced third term bid June 2025.

profile.religion: Jewish (Reform; family of Ukrainian Jewish descent; instrumental in building Illinois Holocaust Museum)
profile.education: B.A. Political Science, Duke University (1987); J.D., Northwestern University School of Law (1990)
profile.background: Billionaire businessman, entrepreneur, and venture capitalist from the Pritzker family (Hyatt Hotels); became 43rd governor of Illinois in 2019.

candidates.name: Dick Durbin
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 0% session, 4% lifetime. NRA F. Catholic who breaks with Church on abortion (was pro-life early career). #2 Senate Democrat (Minority Whip). Not seeking reelection 2026 — 'time to pass the torch.' Authored DREAM Act. Longest-serving Illinois senator in history. Score 2/70. His retirement creates an open seat in 2026 — one of the most important Senate races.

profile.education: B.S. Foreign Service, Georgetown University (1966); J.D., Georgetown University Law Center (1969)
profile.background: Former Senate Majority Whip and longest-serving senator in Illinois history. Served in U.S. House from 1983-1997. Former attorney and legislative counsel to Lieutenant Governor.
profile.office: 711 Hart Senate Office Building

candidates.name: Tammy Duckworth
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 0%, NRA F. Combat veteran who lost both legs in Iraq — Purple Heart. First senator to give birth in office. Deist (mother Buddhist, father Southern Baptist). Born in Thailand to American father. Leading advocate for IVF and reproductive freedom. Her combat veteran status gives her moral authority on defense and gun issues that is hard for opponents to challenge. Score 2/70.

profile.religion: Deist (mother is Buddhist; father was Southern Baptist; personally identifies as deist — believes in God through reason and nature, not organized religion)
profile.birthplace: Bangkok, Thailand (American father, Thai-Chinese mother; raised in Hawaii and Southeast Asia)
profile.education: B.A. Political Science, University of Hawaii at Manoa (1989); M.A. International Affairs, George Washington University (1992); Ph.D. candidate, Capella University (Human Services)
profile.background: Iraq War veteran and Black Hawk helicopter pilot who lost both legs in combat. Purple Heart recipient. Former Illinois VA director and U.S. House member. First Thai-American U.S. Senator.
profile.office: 524 Hart Senate Office Building

candidates.name: Robert Peters
candidates.notes: Heritage Action 0%/6% lifetime. NRA F. Episcopalian. Navy Reserve veteran. Announced retirement Jan 2025 — not seeking reelection. His open seat creates a major 2026 Michigan Senate battleground. Rated most effective senator 2021-22. Quiet workhorse rather than media figure. Score 2/70.

profile.education: B.A. Political Science, Alma College (1980); M.B.A., University of Detroit Mercy (1984); J.D., Wayne State University Law School (1989); M.A. Philosophy, Michigan State University (2007)
